mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation expectations, as indicated by a 0.5% monthly increase in CPIAUCSL, trigger central bank policy tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals robust supply-side expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window for the combined INDPRO-GDPC1 signal.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market, indicated by a 0.3% monthly decline in UNRATE, reduces wage pressures and improves corporate margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE declines by >0.3%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) reflect elevated consumer confidence and demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through sectoral spillovers.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of periods with HOUST growth > 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A positive correlation between consumer spending (RSAFS) and employment growth (PAYEMS) indicates strong demand-side momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 for the RSAFS-PAYEMS interaction over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising FEDFUNDS and elevated CPIAUCSL signals a monetary tightening cycle with inflation persistence, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where both FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL > 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) in a low-unemployment environment signals sustained labor market strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through corporate hiring optimism.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where UNRATE < 4% and PAYEMS growth > 100k.
